title: Deal Or No Deal: Mullin's Kentish Coronation
title_candidates: Deal Or No Deal: Mullin's Kentish Coronation
Mullin It Over: A Wire-to-Wire Walk Along the Kent Coast
The Sixteenth Hole Club: Alex Baker's Recurring Nightmare
By the Sea, By the Sea: TEG 14 on the Kent Links
theme: David Mullin leads from gun to tape across four Kent links, but the margin tells a different story to the contest: a 9-point cushion at halfway is whittled to two by Sunday tea, as Alex Baker mounts a charge undone — twice, on the same hole number — by the par-4 16th.
foreshadow: Plant Alex Baker's quintuple-bogey 10 at the 16th at Royal Cinque Ports (b04) in R1 — a hole number to remember.
Note that Mullin's lead, vast at halfway (9), is not as safe as it looks: he is a man who can run up a 9 (b07) or an 8 (b08) at any moment.
Jon Baker plants himself at the bottom on R1 H4 (b31) and never leaves — set up the Spoon as a four-round vigil.
Hint that Alex Baker's appetite for the spectacular blowup (R1, R2, R2, R3...) is the only thing keeping Mullin honest.

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Mullin seized the lead on R1 H16 (b38) and never surrendered it — leader at every checkpoint (b39, b35, b34, b32). The defining stretch was his 13-point burst on holes 9-12 at Royal Cinque Ports (b16), which built a cushion he'd never need to relinquish. By R2 the gap was a yawning 9. The race only became a race in R4, when Alex Baker's blistering opening (b22) and mid-round surge (b19) shaved Mullin's advantage down — until Baker carded a 10 at the 16th (b02), echoing his R1 collapse. Final margin: two.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: A briefer skirmish: Alex Baker levelled on R1 H6 (b42) before Mullin reasserted on H9 (b40) and walked away. By halfway the Jacket gap was a frankly absurd 12 strokes; despite a quadruple at Prince's 8th (b07) and another at the 10th in R4 (b08), Mullin's gross consistency on links he plainly enjoys delivered a seven-shot win at +68.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: A one-act tragedy. Jon Baker dropped to last on R1 H4 (b31) — a single bogey on the 4th at Deal — and stayed there for the remaining 68 holes. He showed flickers (a tidy three-par run at Deal (b30), a sparkling 14-point burst at Prince's (b06)), but each was punctured by ruinous holes — the quad at Prince's 13th (b13), the 17th triple that ended a clean run (b24), and an 8 up the last on Sunday (b14) to round it off in style.

headline_candidates: Deal Me In: Mullin Lays Down a Marker
The 16th Begins Its Work on Alex Baker
Royal Cinque Ports, Royal Cinque Points: Mullin 40
rounds.chosen_headline: Deal Me In: Mullin Lays Down a Marker
rounds.angle: Mullin's 13-point salvo from the 9th (b16) establishes him at the top of both boards by the close; Alex Baker's first 16th-hole horror (b04) hints at trouble to come; Jon Baker tumbles to last on the 4th (b31) and stays there.

headline_candidates: Littlestone, Big Lead: Mullin Out to Nine
Alex Baker Bleeds Out on the Marsh
Two Birdies, One Lifeline: Mullin Stops the Bleeding
rounds.chosen_headline: Littlestone, Big Lead: Mullin Out to Nine
rounds.angle: Mullin posts 41 and stretches the Trophy gap to a daunting nine; Alex Baker haemorrhages twelve shots in four holes early (b29) with two more quads to follow (b10, b11); Mullin's birdie recoveries at the 7th and 18th (b20, b21) keep the round tidy.

headline_candidates: Prince's and the Pauper Field: Mullin Cruises
A Quad at the 8th — and Still He Leads
Back Nine, Front Foot: Mullin's Split Decision
rounds.chosen_headline: Prince's and the Pauper Field: Mullin Cruises
rounds.angle: Mullin survives a quadruple bogey at the 8th (b07) and still wins the day, transformed on the inward half (b18) and signing off with a 13-point finish (b17); Jon Baker's clean run dies at the 17th (b24); Gregg Williams flatters then deceives (b33, b28).

headline_candidates: Sixteenth Time Unlucky: Alex Baker's 10 Hands Mullin the Trophy
Eight Becomes Two: The Charge That Wasn't
A 10 on the 16th. Again. Of Course.
rounds.chosen_headline: Sixteenth Time Unlucky: Alex Baker's 10 Hands Mullin the Trophy
rounds.angle: Alex Baker erodes Mullin's lead with a hot start (b22) and a steady middle (b19) — until the 16th, where he runs up a 10 for the second time this week (b02, mirroring b04), ending the contest; Mullin's own quad at the 10th (b08) and Jon Baker's closing 8 (b14) cannot disguise the result.

players.arc: Wire-to-wire double winner: a man who led every checkpoint of both competitions, weathered his own blowups (b07, b08), and converted a commanding halfway lead into a two-shot Trophy win and seven-shot Jacket — his third best Trophy finish ever.

The nearly man and the entertainer-in-chief: runner-up in the Trophy, but the leading authority on the par-4 (and par-5) blowup, with five quadruple-or-worse holes including matching 10s at the 16th in R1 and R4 (b04, b02).

Spoon-holder by attrition: bottom from R1 H4 (b31) to the final putt, despite the week's single most thrilling stretch — a 14-point flurry at Prince's on Sunday (b06) — undone by an 8 at the last (b14).

Mid-pack and middling: capable of three pars in a row (b33), capable of a 9 on the 3rd at Deal (b15); the round-three steady run that ended with a triple at the 12th (b28) the neatest summary of his week.

cuts: b32 (round-leadership beats — fold into narrative, don't list)
b42 (early R1 lead changes — routine while the field is bunched)
b37 (lower-tier stretches, redundant with b29 and b16)
b27 (collapse-after-steady beats that overlap with b02 and b14)
b23 (Mullin's R1 wobble — minor, swamped by b16)
venue_notes: Frame the week as TEG's 2nd visit to Kent (2021) and a four-round march along the south-east coast: Royal Cinque Ports at Deal (R1, 2nd TEG round here — 'a formidable championship links' and two-time Open host); Littlestone on the Romney Marshes (R2, 2nd visit, Laidlaw Purves of Royal St George's, refined by Braid and MacKenzie); then a Prince's double at Sandwich on the Shore/Dunes routing (R3 2nd visit, R4 3rd visit). Lean into links wind, Open Championship pedigree at Deal, and the rare luxury of back-to-back days on the same loop at Prince's to underline how Alex Baker contrived to detonate on the 16th hole at two entirely different courses.
